text.title: Go HTTP handler rules: framework-mixing, testing, agent gotchas
text.summary: Rules preventing the most common Go HTTP handler mistakes: mixed framework signatures, raw err.Error() in responses, missing server timeouts, and test generation gaps.

rule.statement: Lock the router choice (Gin, Echo, Chi, stdlib) in the system prompt for any LLM session. Reject any file where Gin and Echo imports coexist. Add a CI lint check for the unwanted import.
rule.rationale: LLMs mix handler signatures when context windows overflow. func(c *gin.Context) and func(c echo.Context) error are incompatible; mixed files produce confusing compile errors.

antipattern.description: c.JSON(status, gin.H{"error": err.Error()}) for error responses.
antipattern.reason: Exposes database DSNs, constraint names, and internal hostnames. Use the RFC 7807 error mapping from error-handling/ and return via c.Error(appErr).

rule.statement: Set ReadHeaderTimeout, WriteTimeout, and IdleTimeout on http.Server. Frameworks do not set these; a server without timeouts is vulnerable to slowloris attacks and resource exhaustion.
rule.rationale: A client that never finishes sending headers holds a goroutine and file descriptor indefinitely. ReadHeaderTimeout terminates such connections.

rule.statement: Test handlers with httptest.NewRecorder + router.ServeHTTP. Cover both happy-path and unhappy-path (missing required field, invalid type, oversized body). Do not spin a real server for unit tests.
rule.rationale: httptest tests run in parallel, start instantly, and exercise the full middleware chain. Agents generating only happy-path tests miss the most common field-validation regressions.

antipattern.description: Forgetting defer r.Body.Close() in stdlib handlers.
antipattern.reason: Without Close(), the underlying TCP connection is not returned to the pool, leaking file descriptors under sustained load.

antipattern.description: Creating validator.New() per request instead of once at package level.
antipattern.reason: validator.New() is expensive (reflection over all struct tags). A per-request instance adds measurable latency under load. Cache at package or handler-struct level.

antipattern.description: Using path param accessor from the wrong router: c.Param("id") in Chi or r.PathValue("id") in Gin.
antipattern.reason: Each router has its own path-param API. Wrong accessor returns an empty string, silently breaking 404 logic and tests.
